cards.name: Best Artisan Empanadas
cards.address: 533 E Campbell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Kick off the day at Best Artisan Empanadas on Campbell Ave. Grab a couple fresh empanadas and a coffee before they sell out — this place closes at 3 PM so morning is the move.
cards.why: A rainy Tuesday morning calls for something warm and quick before you head out.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: The Triton Museum of Art in Santa Clara is free and genuinely great. Walk the sculpture garden if the rain lets up, or spend the full two hours inside with the contemporary exhibits.
cards.why: Free art museum plus a covered indoor option on a grey day is a no-brainer.

cards.name: Smoking Pig BBQ
cards.address: 1144 N 4th St, San Jose, CA 95112, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Smoking Pig BBQ for a brisket plate and mac and cheese. It's a proper sit-down BBQ lunch — order big and don't rush it.
cards.why: Cold and rainy is basically BBQ weather.

cards.name: Colibri Pottery Studio Campbell
cards.address: 1033 S Bascom Ave, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Colibri Pottery Studio on Bascom Ave is a hands-on pottery session that's genuinely fun for adults. Get your hands in some clay and make something weird.
cards.why: It's a rainy afternoon and making pottery is way more satisfying than scrolling your phone indoors.

cards.name: Arawan Thai Massage and Spa
cards.address: 14107 Winchester Blvd Suite J, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down at Arawan Thai Massage and Spa in Los Gatos. Even a quick 60-minute session is enough to reset before dinner.
cards.why: You'll want this after a full day, and they're open until 8.

cards.name: Santa Clara University Baseball vs Stanford
cards.blurb: Santa Clara University Baseball takes on Stanford tonight at 6 PM. College baseball is low-key and fun, and the SCU campus is a nice setting for a game.
cards.why: SCU vs Stanford is a genuinely good rivalry matchup and it's free.

cards.name: Beat The Lock Escape Room
cards.address: 2131 The Alameda A, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Cap the night at Beat The Lock Escape Room on The Alameda. Book a room for two and see if you can crack it before time runs out.
cards.why: It runs until 10 PM and is a great way to end the night without just landing at a bar.

cards.name: The Tech Interactive
cards.address: 201 S Market St, San Jose, CA 95113, USA
cards.blurb: Everything at The Tech is hands-on — kids can design, build, and experiment their way through two floors of interactive exhibits, and the IMAX theater is worth adding on if something good is playing.
cards.why: Rainy Tuesday morning and a giant indoor museum full of things to touch and break (on purpose) is a perfect match.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Explore rotating contemporary exhibitions and the permanent collection at this welcoming community art museum — a great indoor option when the rain picks up after your morning at The Tech.
cards.why: It keeps the cultural momentum going while staying dry and central to your route.

cards.name: Main Street Cupertino
cards.address: 19401 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Browse a lively outdoor-covered shopping strip with cafés, quick bites, and shops — good for grabbing a snack and letting the kids burn off some energy before the afternoon arts class.
cards.why: It bridges the afternoon gap nicely and gives everyone a chance to stretch and browse before heading to Los Gatos.

cards.name: Oyster Shell Decoupage Art for Kids (Ages 7-12)
cards.address: 110 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030
cards.blurb: Kids ages 7-12 get to do oyster shell decoupage art at a real workshop on Main Street in Los Gatos. Bring them ready to get a little messy and walk out with something they actually made.
cards.why: A free, structured art activity for kids on a rainy afternoon is exactly the kind of thing that saves the day.

cards.name: Campbell Creamery
cards.address: 267 E Campbell Ave ste a, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Stop into Campbell Creamery for a scoop before dinner. The flavors rotate and the portions are solid — kids pick their cone, parents survive the day.
cards.why: A quick scoop before dinner is a classic move and the kids will not complain.

cards.name: Baby Bouncers Lapsit for Prewalkers
cards.blurb: Join us on Tuesday mornings for Baby Bouncers Lapsit (for non-walking babies) up to 12 months old. This is a baby and caregivers program with an early literacy
cards.why: A solid pick to break up the day.

cards.name: Vasona Lake County Park
cards.address: 333 Blossom Hill Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the evening with a peaceful stroll around the lake — the paths are gentle and the scenery is calming even on a cool, drizzly night after a full day out.
cards.why: It's a calm, low-key way to close out the day close to home in Campbell.

cards.name: Los Gatos Coffee Roasting
cards.address: Los Gatos Coffee Roasting Co.
cards.blurb: Start the morning here with a proper cup from their roastery — grab a seat inside and let the coffee do its job before the day kicks off.
cards.why: Rainy Tuesday mornings were made for a slow coffee in a good local spot.

cards.name: Behavioral Health Navigators
cards.address: 110 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030
cards.blurb: Drop in at 110 E Main St for the Behavioral Health Navigators session — a free community resource worth knowing about, especially if you or someone you know has been looking for support or guidance.
cards.why: It's free, it's today, and it's the kind of thing that actually matters.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Head over to the Triton Museum of Art in Santa Clara — walk the galleries at your own pace, then spend some time in the sculpture garden even if the weather's a little gray.
cards.why: Free world-class contemporary art and zero crowds on a rainy weekday.

cards.name: Yiassoo
cards.address: 10660 S De Anza Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Lunch at Yiassoo on De Anza — Greek comfort food done right, with solid plates that hold up on a cold afternoon.
cards.why: A warm sit-down lunch is exactly right when it's 56 degrees and drizzling.

cards.name: Open To Serenity
cards.address: 116 E Campbell Ave Suite 7, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Book a massage at Open To Serenity on E Campbell Ave — it's a legit spot for a proper session when you want to actually unwind mid-afternoon.
cards.why: A rainy Tuesday afternoon with no kids is the perfect excuse to actually use a massage appointment.

cards.name: 1-on-1 Tech Help
cards.address: 1600 Hopkins Dr San Jose
cards.blurb: Get free one-on-one support with everyday tech questions. Our Tech Mentor assistance can include but not limited to: Basic smartphone, tablet, and computer help
cards.why: A solid pick to break up the day.

cards.name: Smoking Pig BBQ
cards.address: 1144 N 4th St, San Jose, CA 95112, USA
cards.blurb: Dinner at Smoking Pig BBQ — get the brisket plate and the mac, eat inside where it's warm, and don't rush it.
cards.why: There's no better call for dinner on a cold night than a proper BBQ spot with low prices and serious food.

cards.name: Castro Street, Mountain View
cards.address: Castro Street, Mountain View
cards.blurb: Start the morning on Castro Street and grab coffee and breakfast from one of the cafes along the strip before the rain picks up.
cards.why: It's the heart of Mountain View and a solid way to ease into the day.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: The State of Illusion Exhibition is showing at Santa Clara University today — duck inside and let the art do its thing while it's wet and grey out.
cards.why: Free, indoors, and a genuinely good reason to get out of the house on a rainy morning.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: The Triton Museum of Art is free and has both contemporary galleries and a sculpture garden — spend a little time inside with the rotating exhibits.
cards.why: It's one of those places locals keep sleeping on, and rainy days are honestly perfect for it.

cards.name: Smoking Pig BBQ
cards.address: 1144 N 4th St, San Jose, CA 95112, USA
cards.blurb: Dig into slow-smoked BBQ brisket, ribs, or pulled pork with classic sides like mac and cheese and coleslaw — a hearty midday meal to power you through the afternoon.
cards.why: After a morning of art and culture, a casual BBQ spot is the perfect way to refuel before your hands-on sewing class.

cards.name: Sewing with FabMo: Fabric Flowers
cards.address: 585 Franklin St, Mountain View, CA 94041
cards.blurb: Sewing with FabMo has a fabric flowers workshop tonight in Mountain View at 585 Franklin St — drop in and make something with your hands for a couple of hours.
cards.why: It's free, creative, and a fun thing to do on a grey afternoon before dinner.

cards.name: LA ViE MD Los Gatos
cards.address: 652 N Santa Cruz Ave Suite A, Los Gatos, CA 95030, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down at LA ViE MD in Los Gatos with a quick spa treatment or facial before dinner — book ahead if you can.
cards.why: A rainy Tuesday is basically the universe telling you to treat yourself.

cards.name: Smash City Rage Rooms
cards.address: 858 Park Ave, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Pick your weapon — a baseball bat, a sledgehammer, or a crowbar — and smash your way through glassware, electronics, and more inside a fully kitted-out rage room.
cards.why: After a full day of creativity and wellness, letting loose in a rage room is a ridiculously fun way to cap off the evening.

cards.name: Castro Street, Mountain View
cards.address: Castro Street, Mountain View
cards.blurb: Start the morning on Castro Street and grab breakfast or coffee from one of the cafes along the strip — good food, easy parking, and the kids can stretch their legs on the sidewalk while you wake up.
cards.why: It's the natural home base for a Mountain View day and a solid way to kick things off.

cards.name: The Tech Interactive
cards.address: 201 S Market St, San Jose, CA 95113, USA
cards.blurb: The Tech Interactive is basically a giant hands-on playground for curious kids — let them build, experiment, and run wild through the exhibits while you trail behind trying to keep up.
cards.why: On a rainy 62-degree day, this is exactly where you want to be with kids.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: San Pedro Square Market lets everyone pick their own lunch from a bunch of different vendors under one roof — tacos, banh mi, ramen, whatever sounds good, all in the same spot.
cards.why: No negotiating with picky eaters when there are a dozen options in one building.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: The Triton Museum of Art in Santa Clara is free and genuinely worth the stop — wander the sculpture garden with the kids, then head inside for the contemporary galleries.
cards.why: Free admission and a sculpture garden that kids can actually move around in makes this an easy yes.

cards.name: Los Gatos Creek Trail
cards.address: 1250 Dell Ave, Campbell, CA 95008, USA
cards.blurb: Stretch your legs along this paved creekside trail — grab a covered section near the trailhead and enjoy the greenery even if the drizzle picks up.
cards.why: A relaxed outdoor walk is a great breather between museum time and the evening arts workshop.

cards.name: Oyster Shell Decoupage Art for Kids (Ages 7-12)
cards.address: 110 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030
cards.blurb: This Oyster Shell Decoupage Art class at 110 E Main St in Los Gatos is free and designed for kids ages 7 to 12 — they get to make something they'll actually want to bring home.
cards.why: A free, structured art activity on a rainy afternoon is a gift.

cards.name: Lunardi's
cards.address: 720 Blossom Hill Rd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Pick up some fresh provisions, local snacks, or a bottle of wine to take home from this well-stocked neighborhood grocery tucked right in Los Gatos.
cards.why: It's a convenient and practical wind-down stop right after the kids' art class wraps up.

cards.name: Chromatic Coffee
cards.address: Chromatic Coffee, San Jose
cards.blurb: Start your rainy Tuesday with a warm specialty coffee and maybe a pastry at this San Jose roaster known for precise, thoughtful brews. Settle in at a table, listen to the rain, and ease into the day at your own pace.
cards.why: A cozy local coffee stop is the perfect low-key way to kick off a mellow, indoor-leaning day.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Browse the stalls at this covered downtown market and put together a lunch from the mix of vendors — think tacos, sandwiches, or a craft beer. The indoor layout makes it a natural shelter from the April rain.
cards.why: It fills the midday meal gap and keeps you close to the Music Building stop that follows right after.

cards.name: Voice Area Listening Hour at Music Building
cards.blurb: Free listening session in the SJSU Music Building. Show up, sit down, and hear what the voice students have been working on.
cards.why: It's free, it's low-key, and it makes for a genuinely interesting midday stop.

cards.name: Ban Sabai Thai Massage and Spa
cards.address: 108 Race St, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Book a massage and let the afternoon melt off. Good call on a cold, grey, rainy day when you have no excuse not to.
cards.why: A 57-degree rainy day was made for exactly this kind of stop.

cards.name: Mojobreak Sports Cards
cards.address: 2060 Duane Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95054, USA
cards.blurb: Browse the sports card shop and see what's on the tables. Even if you're not a collector, it's a fun hour of nostalgia.
cards.why: Low-key, covered, and a nice way to fill the pre-dinner gap.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the evening with a proper dinner back at San Pedro Square Market — the vendor variety means everyone at the table finds something they like, from burgers to ramen.
cards.why: It's a relaxed, no-reservation spot that lands right after Mojobreak and keeps the evening anchored downtown.

cards.name: The Tech Interactive
cards.address: 201 S Market St, San Jose, CA 95113, USA
cards.blurb: Cap the night with a wander through The Tech Interactive's hands-on science and innovation exhibits — there's always something to tinker with, whether it's robotics, AI demos, or design challenges.
cards.why: It's a fun, dry indoor activity that gives the evening a genuinely engaging finish without requiring much planning.

cards.name: The Tech Interactive
cards.address: 201 S Market St, San Jose, CA 95113, USA
cards.blurb: Head straight to The Tech Interactive and let the kids loose. Everything here is hands-on, from design challenges to body exhibits, and the IMAX theater is a solid bonus if something is showing.
cards.why: Rain is basically a non-issue inside, and kids genuinely go wild in this place.

cards.name: Baby Bouncers Lapsit for Prewalkers
cards.blurb: Baby Bouncers Lapsit is a library program designed for babies who aren't walking yet. Songs, rhymes, and bouncing — it's short, sweet, and totally free.
cards.why: If you've got a little one in the mix, this is a perfect low-key slot before the lunch rush.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Walk over to San Pedro Square Market and let everyone pick their own thing. Tacos, ramen, banh mi, pizza — there's something for every picky eater under one roof.
cards.why: It's the easiest family lunch in downtown San Jose, no arguing over a single menu required.

cards.name: Oyster Shell Decoupage Art for Kids (Ages 7-12)
cards.address: 110 E Main St, Los Gatos, CA 95030
cards.blurb: Take the kids to this oyster shell decoupage art class in Los Gatos. Ages 7-12 get to make something they'll actually want to bring home.
cards.why: A hands-on art activity in the afternoon is a great way to burn off some energy without going outside in the rain.

cards.name: Smoking Pig BBQ
cards.address: 1144 N 4th St, San Jose, CA 95112, USA
cards.blurb: Settle in at Smoking Pig BBQ for dinner. Get the brisket plate, pile on the sides, and don't skip the mac and cheese.
cards.why: It's casual, hearty, and absolutely the right call after a full day with kids.

cards.name: Japantown San Jose
cards.address: Japantown, San Jose
cards.blurb: Wind down with a walk through Japantown. Pick up mochi at Okayama Kobo or grab something sweet to finish the night on a good note.
cards.why: It's a low-key, fun way to end the evening without keeping the kids out too late.

cards.name: The Farm
cards.address: 2450 Park Blvd, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning here with the kids — The Farm opens at 7 and it's a great low-key spot to fuel up before the day gets going.
cards.why: A chill breakfast with the family sets a good pace for a rainy Tuesday.

cards.name: Cantor Arts Center
cards.address: Cantor Arts Center, Stanford University
cards.blurb: Wander through Stanford's free art museum, where rotating galleries mix modern works with classical sculpture and a serene outdoor courtyard that's perfect for a rainy morning.
cards.why: A covered cultural detour right on campus keeps you dry while giving your morning a creative spark between breakfast and lunch.

cards.name: Oren's Hummus
cards.address: 261 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Grab a bowl of hummus and some pita for the kids — it's fast, filling, and genuinely one of the better lunch values on University Ave.
cards.why: Quick, cheap, and the kids will actually eat it.

cards.name: LEGO Tuesdays!
cards.address: 270 Forest Ave. Palo Alto
cards.blurb: Head over to the LEGO Tuesdays drop-in at 270 Forest Ave and let the kids build whatever they want — it's free and a perfect rainy afternoon activity.
cards.why: Free LEGO time for kids on a rainy Tuesday is basically a perfect situation.

cards.name: Color Me Mine
cards.address: 321 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Walk into Color Me Mine on University Ave and pick out a piece of pottery to paint together — the kids can go as creative as they want and you pick it up fired later.
cards.why: It's hands-on, indoors, and the kids stay genuinely engaged the whole time.

cards.name: Pints of Joy
cards.address: 717 E El Camino Real #6, Sunnyvale, CA 94087, USA
cards.blurb: Swing by Pints of Joy in Sunnyvale for a scoop or two before dinner — the flavors are creative and the kids will love it even on a cool day.
cards.why: Ice cream after a craft project is a hard tradition to argue with.

cards.name: Watercourse Way Bath House Spa
cards.address: 165 Channing Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Wind down the day in one of the private soaking tubs or relaxing communal baths at this beloved neighborhood bathhouse, where you can let the tension from a full day of activities melt away.
cards.why: After a packed day of activities, a warm soak is exactly the right way to close things out on a cool, rainy evening.
